title: OSCORE integration missing in Python CoAP transport
description:
VIOLATION of spec/06-security.md 8.7: Python CoAP transport (transport.py, node_channel.py) sends plaintext datagrams without OSCORE protection. The OSCORE library exists (lichen.crypto.oscore.MemorySecurityContext) but is not integrated.

FIX REQUIRED:
1. Modify LichenTransport.send() to protect messages with OSCORE before encoding
2. Modify LichenTransport._on_datagram() to unprotect OSCORE messages
3. NodeChannel needs similar integration
4. EDHOC handshake must run before first protected message exchange

Files to modify:
- python/src/lichen/coap/transport.py
- python/src/lichen/coap/node_channel.py

Reference implementation: rust/lichen-node/src/secure.rs
close_reason: Implemented SecureDatagramChannel wrapper for OSCORE protection per spec 8.7. Created python/src/lichen/coap/secure.py with: OscoreContextStore for managing security contexts, TofuPeerResolver for TOFU key pinning, SecureDatagramChannel that wraps any DatagramChannel with transparent OSCORE protect/unprotect. Contexts can be pre-provisioned via add_context() or established via EDHOC (interface provided, full EDHOC CoAP exchange deferred to separate bead). Added 18 tests in tests/coap/test_secure.py.
